scenario: Quanta Fintech is migrating 1100 instances of its Camunda 8.8 disbursement process. The new process model adds a new message catch event on the existing service task and removes an old timer catch event that is no longer needed. The team plans the catch-event mapping instructions inside the migration plan.
question: How are the new target catch event and the removed source catch event handled by the mapping instructions during migration?

options.text: The unmapped new target catch event causes a new subscription to be opened on migration, and the unmapped source catch event has its existing subscription closed during the migration.
The unmapped new target catch event is ignored at migration time, and the unmapped source catch event keeps its subscription open as a fallback so no events are lost during migration.
The unmapped new target catch event requires a manual SubscribeEvent call after migration, and the unmapped source catch event is automatically rolled into the new target catch event.
The unmapped new target catch event raises an incident during migration, and the unmapped source catch event is automatically converted into a boundary event on the active scope task.

a.text: Correct. If a target catch event is not the target of a mapping, a new subscription is opened on migration; if a source catch event is not mapped, its subscription is closed during migration.

b.text: Incorrect. Unmapped target catch events are not ignored: new subscriptions are opened for them. Unmapped source catch events do not stay open as a fallback; their subscriptions are closed.

c.text: Incorrect. No manual SubscribeEvent call is needed; subscriptions for unmapped target catch events are opened automatically as part of the migration.

d.text: Incorrect. Unmapped target catch events do not raise an incident, and source catch events are not converted into boundary events; the engine opens or closes subscriptions based on whether mappings are present.

explanation: Catch event handling during migration is driven by mapping instructions: mapped catch events have their subscriptions migrated, unmapped target catch events get new subscriptions, and unmapped source catch events have their subscriptions closed.
